title: Max Sum Subarray of Size K
description: Given an array of numbers and an integer k, return the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of length k.
whatShouldHappen: Compute the sum of the first window (first k elements).
Slide the window one step right each iteration.
Add the incoming element and subtract the outgoing element.
Track the maximum window sum seen and return it.
patternExplanation: Instead of recomputing each window sum from scratch (O(n·k)), reuse the previous sum by adding the incoming element and subtracting the outgoing one — O(n) total.

title: Longest Subarray with Sum ≤ K
description: Given an array of non-negative integers and a value k, return the length of the longest contiguous subarray whose sum is at most k.
whatShouldHappen: Use a left and right pointer both starting at 0.
Expand the window by advancing the right pointer and adding to the running sum.
If the sum exceeds k, shrink the window by advancing the left pointer and subtracting.
After each step, update the maximum window length seen.
patternExplanation: Expand with the right pointer and shrink with the left — the window size flexes to maintain a constraint, avoiding the need to restart from every possible left boundary.
